critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Cannot formally specify domain constraints, leading to ad-hoc design
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 1 teaches formal Pre notation: '{Pre: conditions on params}'

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Without formal Post, derivation-driven design is impossible; results in guess-and-test
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 1 teaches: '{Post: relations between params and results}'. Disjunctions → conditionals

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Code appears ad-hoc; no logical path from spec to implementation. Hard to verify, extend, or teach
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 2 'Derivation Over Verification': 'Derive code from postcondition analysis'

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Tests are arbitrary; lack coverage structure. Miss edge cases, don't catch all bugs
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 'Connecting Formal Specs to Tests' maps: precondition-violation → unhappy path, disjunctions → per-branch tests, constructors → base/inductive tests

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Loop semantics unclear; hard to verify correctness or extend loop safely
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 'Iterative Programs' defines loop invariant as: 'condition that holds at start of every iteration'

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: No proof; relying on intuition or testing. Subtle bugs in loop logic go undetected
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 'Verification (Two Phases)' requires: '{I ∧ B} S {I}' — prove loop body preserves invariant

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: No guarantee loop terminates; infinite loops possible. Can't reason about performance or safety
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md defines bounding function t: 'I ∧ B ⟹ t > 0' and '{I ∧ B ∧ t=T} S {t<T}'

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Inductive reasoning weak; base cases not justified. Proof incomplete
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 'Principle of Induction': '[P(base) ∧ ∀x (P(smaller(x)) ⟹ P(x))] ⟹ ∀z P(z)'

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Recursion correctness not proven; relies on hope, not logic
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 'Recursive Programs': assume IH for recursive call, prove it preserves postcondition

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Recursion may not terminate; can't trust implementation on new inputs
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md defines bounding function t(x) for recursion; prove t strictly decreases at each call

critical_failures_without_skill.impact: Ad-hoc test suite; gaps in coverage. Doesn't systematically check postcondition
critical_failures_without_skill.skill_fix: SKILL.md Sec 'Testing Recursive Cases': test direct case, minimal recursive case, general case, plus postcondition branches

skill_strengths: All 3 evals with skill achieved 100% pass rate (15/15 assertions)
WITH_SKILL consistently applies formal notation (Pre/Post, quantifiers, logical predicates)
WITH_SKILL derives code from postcondition analysis, not from intuition
WITH_SKILL proves loop/recursion correctness via invariants and bounding functions
WITH_SKILL derives test cases from specification structure (constructors, disjunctions, quantifiers)
without_skill_weaknesses: Avg 26.7% pass rate (4/15 assertions) — major deficit in formal reasoning
Only implementation-correctness assertions pass reliably (3/3 correct implementations)
WITHOUT_SKILL defaults to: informal explanation + working code + ad-hoc tests
Never states formal Pre/Post, loop invariants, or bounding functions
Tests are examples, not systematically derived from specification structure
gap_analysis.key_insight: The skill teaches a transformative methodology: move from guess-and-verify to specify-and-derive. WITH_SKILL responses show this clearly — code is not written ad-hoc and then justified; it is derived from formal analysis of the postcondition. This produces more correct, maintainable, and verifiable software.
improvement_areas_for_skill: Could include more worked examples of immersion technique (algorithm generalization) — mentioned in SKILL.md but not fully illustrated
Could provide explicit template/checklist for users to follow when applying the methodology
Quantifier splitting (Sec 'Quantifiers and Hidden Operations') could use more application examples in the main workflow
Type signature and algebraic reasoning sections are strong but could emphasize their role in the overall derivation workflow more

notes: ANALYST: The methodical-programming skill is highly effective. WITH_SKILL achieves 100% assertion pass rate across all three evaluations. WITHOUT_SKILL averages 26.7%, failing systematically on formal reasoning assertions (Pre/Post, invariants, termination proofs, test derivation).

ANALYST: The skill successfully teaches specification-first design: code is derived from postcondition analysis, not written ad-hoc. Loop and recursion correctness is proven via invariants and bounding functions, not hoped for. Tests are derived from specification structure (constructors, disjunctions), not written as arbitrary examples.

ANALYST: WITH_SKILL responses are rigorous: each uses formal notation, proves proof obligations, derives tests. WITHOUT_SKILL responses are intuitive and produce correct code, but lack the logical structure needed for complex systems, formal verification, or teaching.

ANALYST: No bugs found in the skill itself. All assertions are well-defined and correctly evaluated. The gap (73.33 percentage points) reflects the skill's genuine value in teaching formal methods, not artificial test design.

ANALYST: Immediate value demonstrated: (1) Binary search specification and derivation catches edge cases (duplicate handling, boundary conditions); (2) Partition function formal invariant and termination proof prevents subtle loop bugs; (3) Merge sort inductive proof confirms correctness, derives comprehensive test suite from algebraic structure.

ANALYST: The skill teaches language-agnostic principles (axiomatic semantics, structural induction, algebraic data types) and applies them consistently. All three evals passed in WITH_SKILL regardless of implementation details, showing genuine principle mastery.
ANALYST: Recommendation: skill is production-ready. Minor enhancements: add checklist template, more immersion examples, tighter integration of quantifier-splitting into main workflow.
